summary: Audience FOB capture of a jazzy Lost Sailor > Saint pause shines amid an otherwise undistinguished show.
review: The recording is a solid audience (FOB) tape that captures the energy and warm, mellow ambience of this late‑'70s era Dead show. Reviewers rave about the rare complete stop between Lost Sailor and the sweet opening of Saint of Circumstance, and they give Black Peter a “smoking” nod, but most feel the remainder of the concert drags and lacks distinction.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed – standout moments are praised while the bulk of the set is considered undistinguished; recording quality is generally good for an audience tape, leading to a moderate overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FOB tape praised for energy and clarity in the highlighted sections; overall sound described as good with typical audience‑mic ambience, no major dropouts reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The pause between Lost Sailor and Saint of Circumstance is a highlight, but reviewers note the rest of the set feels undistinguished and loses steam.

band_member_comments.Drums: Drums receive a brief positive nod in the jam segment after Saint.
